hint.semantic: Embed server-side (GPU MultimodalEmbeddingRunner). Fails if not deployed.
hint.hybrid: Vector + BM25 fusion. Embed client-side (embedding worker required).

errorHint.shortQuery: Try a longer search term, or combine several words. To search with short terms only, use Keyword search.
errorHint.embeddingWorker: Could not run {{mode}} search. Check the embedding model or worker readiness in Settings.

delete.title: Delete this thread?
delete.message: This deletes the thread and its conversation messages. It cannot be undone. Generated summaries and reflections remain, so delete them individually from their screens if unwanted.

search.noHitsDesc: Try a different period or search mode

periodic.title: Periodic tasks
periodic.subtitle: Schedule Lookback summary generation with conductor

intro.title: Schedule
intro.desc: The local sidecar's conductor runs the Lookback summary-generation workflow on a cron schedule.

empty.title: No periodic tasks yet
empty.desc: Use the New button to add a summary-generation schedule.

delete.title: Delete periodic task
delete.message: Delete {{name}}. This action cannot be undone.

cancel.title: Stop execution
cancel.message: Send a stop request to the running job for {{name}}.

validation.sourceInvalid: Choose source from codex / claude-code / codex + claude-code
validation.lookbackRange: Enter lookback days as 1 or more
validation.intervalRange: Enter the interval as 1 or more
validation.monthlyDayRange: Enter the monthly day in the range 1-28

personality.title: Personality
personality.forceReextractHint: Force re-extract: re-extracts all threads including those that already have signals (also disables the target_signal_count early stop). Use this to apply prompt changes to historical data.
personality.forceReextractConfirm: Enabling Force re-extract re-extracts all existing signals as well. This puts heavy load on the LLM and may take time depending on the volume. Proceed?
personality.mergeOnlyHint: Runs only user-personality-merge. Skips per-thread re-extraction and regenerates the profile from existing signals (for recovery when only the merge failed, e.g. due to rate limits).
personality.statSignalsHint: Show the list of backing signals
personality.emptyDesc: It is generated when thread-personality-batch → user-personality-merge run after an import completes. If signals already exist but no profile appears, you can recover with "Merge only".
personality.deleteProfileConfirmMessage: Deletes the merged profile. Signals and source threads remain. You can rebuild it with "Generate" again.
personality.signalsEmptyDesc: Signals are extracted when thread-personality-batch runs
personality.deleteSignalConfirmMessage: Deletes this personality signal. The source thread remains. The change is not reflected in the merged profile until the next generation.

empty.line1: Answers by RAG search over your past threads, summaries, and raw logs.
empty.line2: e.g. "What did we discuss about caching recently?" / "When did I write that reflection?"

reflections.title: Reflections
reflections.intentPlaceholder: Natural-language search by intent (e.g. how to fix flaky tests)
reflections.intentTitle: Semantic search over intent_embedding (requires GPU embedding worker)

empty.title: No reflections yet
empty.desc: Generated automatically on import, or run once from the "Generate reflection" button above

delete.title: Delete this reflection?
delete.message: This deletes the reflection. The source thread is not removed. You can recreate it with "Generate reflection".

summaries.title: Summaries
summaries.generateTitle: Generate summaries (pick granularity/period in the dialog)

calendar.selectPrompt: Select a day/week/month to show its summary

delete.title: Delete this summary?
delete.message: This deletes the summary. The source thread and conversation are not removed. You can recreate it with "Generate".

settingsSaveBar.resetWarning: ⚠ The embedding model/dimension will change. The existing vectordb will be reset (evacuated/deleted) and all embeddings must be regenerated (tens of minutes to hours).

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.desc: Connection / Embedding model / MCP server / Data location / Delete all data. Low-frequency, high-impact settings.
advanced.notice: These are low-frequency, high-impact settings. You can change the connection target, embedding model, MCP server, and data location, and delete all data.

vectordbBackup.evacuated: The existing vectordb was moved aside to:
vectordbBackup.manualDelete: To delete it manually, run rm -rf on the path above.

modelStatus.retryTitle: Re-runs plugin staging and sidecar startup
modelStatus.preparingHintPre: The model is downloaded from Hugging Face the first time a {{purpose}} job runs (a few to tens of minutes depending on your connection).
modelStatus.preparingHintPost: to start a job and begin the download. It changes to "Ready" once complete.
modelStatus.readyHint: The model is cached and {{purpose}} is available.
modelStatus.failedDefault: The model could not be prepared.
modelStatus.failedHint: If the sidecar won't start or the plugin (dylib) is missing, check your environment and restart the app.

llm.title: LLM Provider
llm.desc: Choose the LLM backend for summaries, reflections, personality, and chat. External API auto-detects the provider from the model name (gpt-* = OpenAI, claude-* = Anthropic, gemini-* = Gemini, etc.).
llm.deleteKeyTitle: Deletes the Keychain API key on save
llm.deleteKeyWarning: Saving will delete the API key from the Keychain.
llm.apiKeyHint: The API key is stored in the macOS Keychain. Changing the model name or key restarts the sidecar.
llm.proxyNotePre: When using a non-OpenAI model via an OpenAI-compatible proxy such as LiteLLM, prefix the model name with
llm.proxyNotePost: . Without the prefix, the provider is inferred from the model name and a protocol incompatible with the proxy is used.
llm.genParamsHintPre: Max tokens / Temperature apply only to
llm.genParamsHintPost: . The summary / reflection / personality batches use the YAML defaults (e.g. 50000 for monthly summaries) since their output volume is tuned per purpose. Leave blank to restore the defaults (4000 / 0.3).
llm.embeddingRamUnavailable: Embedding RAM: custom embedding model / LLM + Embedding total unavailable
llm.customHintPost: file name inside it. Only Qwen3.5-family and Gemma 4-family models are supported. Only the format is checked (existence on HF is not verified).
llm.ctxSizeHint: Leave blank to use the preset's recommended value ({{value}})
llm.ctxSizeNote: You can reduce it for memory constraints. The reflection workflow needs roughly 222k of context; smaller values split long summaries.
llm.kvCacheTypeHint: Smaller values reduce RAM use but can affect model quality.
llm.customRamUnavailable: RAM estimates are available only for presets with known model structure.
llm.localHint: Uses a local LLM (llama-cpp). Saving restarts the sidecar automatically; a not-yet-downloaded model is fetched from Hugging Face (the first time takes a few to tens of minutes).
llm.customIncomplete: Enter a valid HF repo and .gguf file name for the custom model.

connection.title: Connection
connection.desc: By default it connects to the bundled sidecar. The actual port is chosen at startup and shown read-only when Local is selected (ARCH-7). Choosing Remote lets you override the target with an existing server's gRPC URL (http/https).

logs.title: Logs
logs.desc: Shows the tail of the app / sidecar logs (NFR-6), for troubleshooting. "app" is Lookback's own log, including import failure reasons.

embedding.title: Embedding model
embedding.desc: The model used to embed Memory / Reflection. If the model or vector dimension changes, the existing index is reset (re-generation required). Changing only dtype / max sequence length / tokenizer keeps the index.
embedding.modelPurpose: embedding for search (Semantic / Hybrid / natural language)
embedding.resetBanner: ⚠ Because the embedding model/dimension changes, all existing embeddings will be reset (moved aside/deleted) and must be re-generated (tens of minutes to hours).
embedding.noResetNote: This change does not alter the model/dimension, so the existing vectordb is kept (no re-generation needed). Saving restarts the sidecar.
embedding.remoteDisabled: Embedding settings can't be changed while connected remotely. The remote memories keeps its own vectordb, so a local switch has no effect.
embedding.tokenizerPlaceholder: Same as HF Repo if blank
embedding.multimodalLabel: Embed text and images in the same vector space (unchecking disables image search)
embedding.customRequired: Custom models require an HF Repo and Vector size.

embeddingIndex.title: Embedding index
embeddingIndex.desc: Status and re-generation of the Memory (summaries / thread bodies) embedding index. This is what search (Semantic / Hybrid / natural language) hits.
embeddingIndex.tableMissing: The memory_vector table has not been created (sidecar not running, or embedding disabled).
embeddingIndex.redispatchTitle: Use this to fill gaps or rebuild after changing the embedding model (this takes a while)
embeddingIndex.redispatchConfirm: This will re-generate embeddings for {{count}} memories. It will take a while. Continue?

mcp.title: MCP Server
mcp.descMid: as an MCP server to external MCP clients (e.g. Claude Desktop). It runs alongside jobworkerp's gRPC, so it doesn't affect the app's existing features. The exposed tools are limited to the function-set
mcp.urlPending: Shown after you save and the sidecar restarts
mcp.clientConfigExample: Example MCP client config (streamable HTTP transport):
mcp.timeoutInvalid: Enter the timeout as an integer between {{min}} and {{max}} seconds.
mcp.restartHint: Saving changes restarts the sidecar (MCP enable/disable is decided at startup).

savingModal.title: Applying settings…
savingModal.descWithVectordbReset: Restarting the sidecar and loading the new settings (including the embedding model). Because this includes a vectordb reset and the first model download, it may take tens of minutes to several hours.
savingModal.desc: Restarting the sidecar and loading the new settings. This takes tens of seconds to a few minutes. The first time may take longer because it includes a model download from Hugging Face.

unsavedDialog.title: You have unsaved changes
unsavedDialog.message: This view has unsaved changes. Discard them and leave? (Cancel to stay)

purge.title: Destructive
purge.desc: Stops the sidecar and deletes everything under the data root. This cannot be undone.
purge.label: Delete all data
purge.warningsAlert:
All data was deleted, but the following warnings occurred:

{{warnings}}
purge.modalBody: The following directory will be permanently deleted:
purge.modalWarning: This cannot be undone. After the sidecar is stopped, the data is deleted.

hfHome.helpGlobal: Uses the shell variable $HF_HOME, or ~/.cache/huggingface if unset (default). Shares the cache with huggingface-cli / transformers / Ollama, avoiding redundant downloads of huge models.
hfHome.helpDataRoot: Stores under <App data dir>/models. It is also removed by "Delete all data", so choose this when you want to manage models exclusively for this app.
hfHome.helpCustom: Specify any absolute path. For placing on an external disk, etc.
hfHome.title: Hugging Face cache (HF_HOME)
hfHome.desc: The cache directory for LLM / Embedding models. It can grow to several to tens of GB.

dataRoot.title: Data location
dataRoot.desc: Where sqlite / LanceDB / plugins / logs are stored. Leave blank to revert to the default ({{defaultRoot}}). Use "Delete all data" to remove everything (ARCH-4 / NFR-5).
dataRoot.createTitle: Creates a new directory at this path (mkdir -p)
dataRoot.saveConfirmChange: This will change the App data dir. The currently open data (sqlite / LanceDB / tonic connection) keeps being used as-is, and the change takes effect from the next app launch. Continue?
dataRoot.saveConfirmReset: This will reset the App data dir to the default. The currently open data (sqlite / LanceDB / tonic connection) keeps being used as-is, and the change takes effect from the next app launch. Continue?
dataRoot.savedMsgChange: From the next launch, {{path}} will be used. Please restart the app.
dataRoot.savedMsgReset: From the next launch, the default location will be used. Please restart the app.

validation.existingRoot: Reusing the existing Lookback data root

desc.qwen3-6-27b-ud-q4-k-xl: The most stable for Japanese summary / reflection / chat quality. Supports long context (262k). The actual RAM estimate changes with ctx_size and KV cache type.
desc.qwen3-6-35b-a3b-ud-iq4-nl: MoE (active 3B). A memory-efficient variant that delivers near-27B-dense quality from a comparatively small gguf. The actual RAM estimate changes with ctx_size and KV cache type.
desc.qwen3-5-9b-ud-q4-k-xl: Lightweight and fast Qwen3.5. Japanese quality sufficient for everyday chat and daily summaries. Lower ctx_size makes it easier to run on 16GB-class machines.
desc.gemma-4-26b-a4b-it-ud-iq4-nl: MoE (active 4B). A mid-size Gemma 4 with good Japanese responses. The actual RAM estimate changes with ctx_size and KV cache type.
desc.gemma-4-e4b-it-ud-q4-k-xl: A lightweight Gemma 4 multimodal variant. Runs in a minimal configuration. Lower ctx_size makes it easier to run in low-memory environments.
desc.gemma-4-e2b-it-ud-q4-k-xl: Default. The lightest Gemma 4 IT preset. A practical local-chat option for smaller RAM environments. Maximum context is 131k.
desc.qwen3-5-122b-a10b-ud-iq4-xs: Largest scale. MoE 122B (active 10B). A flagship aiming for top quality. The gguf itself is large, so this targets high-memory machines.

desc.qwen3-vl-embedding-2b: A multimodal variant that embeds text and images into the same vector space. Use this if you want image search.
desc.qwen3-embedding-0-6b: Default. Lightweight and fast. Quality sufficient for everyday use with a small memory footprint.
desc.qwen3-embedding-4b: Mid-size, high quality. Supports long context (40k); summary and Japanese search accuracy stay stable. Image search is disabled.

import.title: Import session logs
import.llmDownBody: Import can run, but summaries, personality, reflections, and semantic search will not be generated. Make sure <code1>{{pluginPath}}</code1> contains <code2>{{dylib}}</code2> etc. See the Sidecar status in the sidebar for details.
import.plainSourceName: Source name (optional, a-z0-9_- up to 32 chars)
import.errorInvalidSourceName: Source name must be a-z0-9_- up to 32 chars

importToast.head: Importing sessions
importToast.cancelHint: Stop the current step and skip the rest

summaryGen.title: Generate summaries
summaryGen.llmDownBody: Summary generation requires the LLM. Check the Sidecar status in the sidebar.
summaryGen.dependencyHint: Selecting a higher level auto-selects its dependencies (weekly → daily → thread).
summaryGen.rangeHintPerThread: Specify a date range (both empty = all threads)
summaryGen.rangeHintDaily: Specify a date range (both empty = yesterday)
summaryGen.rangeHintWeekly: Specify a week range (both empty = last week)
summaryGen.rangeHintMonthly: Specify a month range (both empty = last month)

labelFilter.title: Filter by label ({{count}})
labelFilter.matchAnyHint: Threads with any of the labels (OR)
labelFilter.matchAllHint: Threads with all of the labels (AND)

threadDetail.noRoleMatch: No messages match the visible roles

leaveGuard.title: You have unsaved changes
leaveGuard.message: You have unsaved settings changes. Discard them and leave? (Cancel keeps you on Settings)

welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.intro: Set up importing your Claude Code / Codex logs to look back on your work.
welcome.note: The data location and models can be changed later from the Settings tab.

dataRoot.title: App data dir
dataRoot.desc: Leave blank to keep the current location. Current:
dataRoot.dialogError: Could not open the directory picker. Enter the path manually. Details: {{error}}

apply.restartDesc: Lookback will restart to apply the new data location.

lancedbSchemaMismatch.title: The embedding vector dimension has changed
lancedbSchemaMismatch.body:
The stored vector DB dimension ({{actualDim}}) does not match the current embedding model dimension ({{expectedDim}}).
This commonly happens after switching the embedding model.

lancedbInitFailed.title: Failed to initialize the vector DB

embeddingDimensionMismatch.title: Embedding model dimension mismatch
embeddingDimensionMismatch.body:
The dimension ({{actualDim}}) reported by the embedding runner ({{runnerName}}) does not match the stored MEMORY_VECTOR_SIZE ({{expectedDim}}).
This commonly happens right after switching models.

mediaConfigConflict.title: Media storage settings conflict
mediaConfigConflict.body:
MEDIA_STORAGE_BACKEND={{backend}} cannot be combined with MEMORY_IMAGE_SEARCH_MODE={{imageSearchMode}}.
Check your .env.

rdbPoolInitFailed.title: Database connection failed

configLoadFailed.title: Failed to load configuration
configLoadFailed.body:
{{component}}: {{message}}
Check the related environment variables (the {{component}} prefix).

evacuate.label: Back up vector DB and restart
evacuate.pending: Backing up the vector DB and restarting the sidecar…

purge.label: Delete vector DB and restart (irreversible)
purge.pending: Deleting the vector DB and restarting the sidecar…

resetEmbedding.label: Reset embedding settings and restart
resetEmbedding.pending: Resetting embedding settings and restarting the sidecar…

bootError.restartFailedFallback: The sidecar could not be restarted.
